---
name: Mechanical Cupcakes OS
type: project
status: Architecture Defined / Ready for Handoff
last_updated: 2026-04-25
---

#{1,6} Mechanical Cupcakes OS — Project Brief

#{1,6} Overview
**Mechanical Cupcakes OS** is a **meta-OS shell** that wraps multiple independent subdomain apps (MVPs/experiments) under a unified visual and interaction layer. Each app remains functionally independent while sharing a persistent top bar, global system agent (**Hoot**), and a directory of available tools.

The shell is an **OS frame**, not just a theme — apps are "programs" running inside this OS.

---

#{1,6} What It Is

**The OS provides:**
- A persistent, glass-like top shell bar (always visible, minimal, non-intrusive)
- **Hoot** — a global system agent that knows about apps but does not operate them
- A directory dropdown listing all available apps and subdomains
- Clean separation: shell owns top bar and global navigation; apps own canvas

**Apps included (Phase 1):**
- **Hoot Dashboard** — Lightweight metrics/data exploration dashboard
- **Pelican** — Interactive recipe library with local agent
- **Love Postcards** — Visual message creation and sharing tool

Each app is an independent MVP that can ship separately while part of the cohesive ecosystem.

---

Core Design Principles

1. **Shell is always visible but minimal.** Thin, glass-like top bar that never disappears or pushes app layout
2. **Shell never constrains app internals.** Apps control their full canvas; shell overlays only at top
3. **Shell owns the top; apps own the canvas.** All app UI is app territory; only top bar is OS
4. **No conflicting chat positions.** Local agents live bottom-right inside apps; Hoot lives only in/under top bar
5. **Apps must work if shell fails.** No hard dependencies on shell JS/CSS
6. **Separation of concern.** Hoot is the OS narrator; local agents are in-app specialists; only one voice active at a time

---

#{1,6} Visual Theme: "Midnight Workshop"

**Vibe:** Dark, calm, slightly magical; light glassmorphism, soft neon accents.  
**Applied to:** Shell only (top bar, Hoot panel, directory). Apps can adopt or ignore.

**Color Tokens (Shell):**
- Base background: #0F1115
- Surface: #1A1F2B
- Accent 1: Electric violet (#7C5CFF)
- Accent 2: Soft teal (#2ED3B7)
- Text: Warm white (#EAEAF0)

**Glass Spec:**
- Top bar background: rgba(20, 22, 30, 0.65)
- Backdrop blur: 8–12px
- Border bottom: 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.08)
- Panels (Hoot, directory): slightly more opaque, soft shadow (0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.35))

---

#{1,6} Global Agent: Hoot

Global Agent: Hoot

#{1,6} Role
- **Global system agent** — lives in the shell, not in app canvas
- **Knows about** apps but does **not operate** them
- **OS narrator** — provides high-level guidance, app discovery, onboarding

#{1,6} Capabilities (What Hoot Can Do)
- Explain what an app is and its purpose
- Provide short onboarding on first load
- Answer high-level technical questions (e.g., "stores data locally?")
- Help navigate between apps (open other tools)
- Introduce local agents and defer to them

#{1,6} Limitations (What Hoot Cannot Do)
- Execute in-app actions ("save recipe", "send postcard", etc.)
- Access user-specific or sensitive data
- Replace or compete with local app agents
- Handle app-specific questions (defer to local agent)

#{1,6} Implementation (Phase 1)
- **Fully scripted** — finite state machine (FSM), not a general chatbot
- **Responses from predefined scripts** based on app briefs and metadata
- **Mantra SDK** for the agent surface; prototype scripts, not free-form LLM
- **Core States:** WELCOME, IDLE, ABOUT_APP, NAVIGATE, DEFLECT_TO_LOCAL_AGENT, UNKNOWN

---

#{1,6} Local Agents (App-Level)

- **Owned by individual apps** (e.g., Pelican has its own agent)
- **Positioned** bottom-right corner of app content
- **Scope** — App-specific actions and deep guidance
- **Phase 1 Behavior** — Not aware of Hoot; Hoot mentions them when relevant
- **Separation Rule** — When a local agent exists and is relevant, Hoot defers; only one voice active

---

Shell Layout Specification

#{1,6} Top Bar
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [🦉] Mechanical Cupcakes | {App Name}        [Directory ▼] [Chat w/ Hoot] │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Components:**
- Owl logo (clickable, opens Hoot panel)
- "Mechanical Cupcakes | {Current App Name}" label (center)
- "Directory" dropdown trigger (right)
- "Chat w/ Hoot" button (right)

**Specifications:**
- Height: 48px
- Horizontal padding: 12–16px
- Always overlays app content; never pushes layout
- Z-Index: 900

#{1,6} Hoot Panel
Opens as dropdown from top bar when:
- Owl logo clicked
- "Chat w/ Hoot" clicked
- On first visit, auto-opens once per app

**Specifications:**
- Width: ~360px
- Max-height: ~70vh with internal scroll
- Anchored under owl logo or Chat button
- Dismiss on outside click or ESC
- Z-Index: 1000
- First-visit flag: localStorage/session per app

**Content:**
- Welcome text (scripted, based on app brief)
- 2–3 suggested prompts
- User input field for follow-up questions
- Scripted responses with app-metadata-driven behavior

#{1,6} Directory Dropdown
Lists all available apps in the ecosystem.

**Specifications:**
- Width: ~280–320px
- Triggered by "Directory ▼" button
- Click entry to navigate to that subdomain (same tab)
- Later: optional search, status badges (alpha/beta/stable)

**Entry Format:**
- Icon + app name
- Short description
- Example: "🪶 Pelican — Interactive recipe library"

---

App Configuration & Metadata

Each app exposes a **project brief** (JSON or config) that Hoot reads at runtime:

```json
{
  "name": "Hoot Dashboard",
  "description": "A lightweight dashboard for monitoring structured data.",
  "purpose": "Quickly understand system activity and metrics.",
  "primaryActions": ["View metrics", "Explore data"],
  "hasLocalAgent": false,
  "welcomeScript": "Welcome to Hoot Dashboard...",
  "suggestedPrompts": [
    "What is this app?",
    "Where should I start?",
    "Show other tools"
  ]
}
```

**Hoot uses this to generate:**
- Welcome message
- Directory description
- "What is this?" explanations
- Whether to defer to local agent
- Suggested prompts for new users

---

#{1,6} Layering & Z-Index Rules

- **Hoot panel:** 1000
- **Top bar:** 900
- **Local agent (in-app):** ~100
- **App content:** 0

---

#{1,6} Motion & Interaction

- **Animations:** 150–250ms ease, soft opacity/translate transitions
- **Hover:** Subtle glow or border-lightening, not aggressive color swaps
- **No full-screen takeovers** from Hoot or shell in Phase 1

---

Phase 1 Definition of Done

For v1 launch, the OS is "done" when:

1. ✅ Shell top bar implemented and injected into all participating subdomains
2. ✅ Directory dropdown lists at least first three apps (Hoot Dashboard, Pelican, Postcards)
3. ✅ Hoot panel:
   - Auto-opens once on first visit per app
   - Uses scripted welcome text based on app metadata
   - Provides 2–3 suggested prompts
   - Responds deterministically per FSM state
4. ✅ Local agents remain bottom-right and visually distinct from Hoot
5. ✅ Apps remain fully functional if shell fails to load

---

#{1,6} Technical Implementation Strategy

**For AntiGravity / Implementation Team:**

1. **Shell Component Architecture** — Reusable component injected across subdomains (app-agnostic)
2. **Hoot Service** — FSM-based script engine (Mantra SDK) that reads app briefs at runtime
3. **IPC Protocol** — Between shell and app (optional; for future two-way communication)
4. **App Metadata Standard** — JSON schema for app briefs; versioned for backwards compatibility
5. **Testing Strategy** — Shell must not break any app; graceful degradation if shell fails

---

#{1,6} Apps in Scope (Subdomain Mapping)

| App | Subdomain | Status | Local Agent |
|---|---|---|---|
| Hoot Dashboard | hoot.mechanicalcupcakes.fun | MVP | No (future) |
| Pelican | pelican.mechanicalcupcakes.fun | Active | Yes (Pelican agent) |
| Love Postcards | love.mechanicalcupcakes.fun | Concept | No (future) |

---

Next Steps (Phase 1 → Phase 2)

1. **Phase 1 Scope:** Scripted Hoot, static directory, top bar injection
2. **Phase 2 (Future):** AI-powered Hoot (full Claude API), dynamic directory, app-to-app hand-offs
3. **Phase 3 (Future):** Hoot persistence (memory of user interactions), advanced routing, multi-app workflows

---

#{1,6} Key References & Related Docs

- **Love Postcards Brief:** PROJECT_BRIEF.md in love.postcards folder
- **Pelican Brief:** PROJECT_BRIEF.md in Pelican Recipe Library folder
- **Scout Protocol Brief:** PROJECT_BRIEF.md in Scout Protocol Garage (separate from OS shell)
- **Freedom Browser Codebase:** ErnestOfGaia/freedom-browser-copy (reference architecture)

---

*Handoff Document | Status: Ready for Design & Implementation | Last updated: 2026-04-25*

#{1,6} Pelican Recipe Library — Project Brief

#{1,6} Overview
An interactive recipe library application with AI agent assistance. Pelican is an active MVP deployed across multiple domains as part of the Mechanical Cupcakes OS ecosystem.

**Primary URL:** https://pelican.ernestofgaia.xyz  
**Alternate URL:** https://pelican.mechanicalcupcakes.fun  
**Evolution:** Pellito Hub (C:\Users\Owner\.claude\websites\Pellito Hub)  
**Source Files:** C:\Users\Owner\.gemini\antigravity\scratch\Pelican Recipe Library

---

#{1,6} What It Does
- Interactive recipe search and browsing
- Local AI agent for recipe-specific guidance and suggestions
- Data persistence and personalization
- Operates as a standalone app with its own features while integrated into Mechanical Cupcakes OS

---

#{1,6} Status & Readiness
- **Current:** Active / test/alternate deployment
- **Domains:** Deployed on both ErnestOfGaia.xyz (primary) and mechanicalcupcakes.fun (secondary/testing)
- **MVP Phase:** Core functionality complete; ongoing improvements

---

Agent Architecture
- **Local Agent:** "Pelican" — owns recipe guidance, cooking tips, ingredient substitutions
- **Positioning:** Bottom-right corner of app canvas
- **Scope:** App-specific actions only; does not interact with global Hoot agent in Phase 1
- **Capabilities:** Search assistance, recipe explanation, cooking advice

---

#{1,6} Technical Context
- **Framework:** [Per pelican.ernestofgaia.xyz documentation]
- **Data:** Recipe database with structured metadata
- **Deployment:** Multi-domain (EoG primary, mechanicalcupcakes.fun secondary)
- **Shell Integration:** Shares Mechanical Cupcakes OS top bar on mechanicalcupcakes.fun deployment

---

#{1,6} Domain Strategy
- **Primary (pelican.ernestofgaia.xyz):** Main branded deployment, core audience
- **Secondary (pelican.mechanicalcupcakes.fun):** Testing/staging for new features, alternate access point
- **Status:** To be clarified which domain is canonical once domain strategy is finalized

---

#{1,6} Key Constraints
- App canvas is fully independent; shell top bar overlays only
- Local agent must remain in bottom-right; no competition with Hoot positioning
- No shared session/auth required between domains (independent deployments)
- Must function if OS shell fails to load

---

Next Steps
1. Clarify primary vs. secondary domain canonical status
2. Align shell integration on mechanicalcupcakes.fun with latest OS spec
3. Document local agent API and scope boundaries
4. Plan Pellito Hub (evolved version) roadmap and release timeline
5. Test agent handoff behavior between domains

---

*Part of Mechanical Cupcakes OS portfolio. Local agent documentation in app repo.*

#{1,6} Love Postcards — Project Brief

#{1,6} Overview
A personal postcard creation and sharing application that allows users to design and send visual messages. Part of the Mechanical Cupcakes OS ecosystem as a subdomain app.

**Live URL:** https://love.mechanicalcupcakes.fun  
**Source:** C:\Users\Owner\.claude\websites\Postcard App  
**Integration:** Mechanical Cupcakes OS shell + Hoot global agent

---

#{1,6} What It Does
- Users create custom visual postcards with text and imagery
- Postcards can be shared or sent
- Operates as an independent MVP under the Mechanical Cupcakes OS umbrella

---

#{1,6} Status & Readiness
- **Current:** Concept phase
- **Next:** Define tech stack, design system, core features list
- **Integration:** Will appear in Mechanical Cupcakes directory and share OS shell bar

---

#{1,6} Technical Context
- **Deployment:** Subdomain under mechanicalcupcakes.fun
- **Shell Integration:** Does not require local agent initially
- **App Independence:** Must function if OS shell fails to load
- **Suggested Stack:** [To be defined during design phase]

---

Key Constraints
- App canvas controls all UI; shell owns only top bar
- Local agent (if added) must live in bottom-right corner of app
- No conflicts with Hoot panel positioning
- Visual theme adoption optional (can use app's own design system)

---

#{1,6} Next Steps
1. Define feature scope (core postcard creation flow)
2. Select tech stack (frontend, design system)
3. Clarify local agent requirement
4. Create wireframes for core user flows
5. Integrate with Mechanical Cupcakes OS shell API

---

*Part of Mechanical Cupcakes OS portfolio. See OS Notes for shell architecture.*

Source Files C:\Users\Owner\.gemini\antigravity\scratch\OCHI_Dashboard
Gemini Project

This Project DNA Brief outlines the core identity and technical framework for the **OCHI Dashboard**, serving as the definitive guide for the Phase 1 Prototype development.

---

#{1,6} Project DNA Brief: OCHI Dashboard (Pacific City Pilot)

#{1,6} 1. Project Essence & Identity

- **Official Name:** OCHI Dashboard (Oregon Coastal Hospitality Intelligence).
    
- **The "Patient Zero" Location:** Pacific City, Tillamook County, OR.
    
- **Primary Goal:** To build a predictive dashboard that correlates external "Gatekeeper" indicators with hospitality performance.
    
- **Operating Context:** A 70% "Weekender" consumer profile, characterized by longer stays and larger groups.
    

#{1,6} 2. The Core Philosophy

- **Design Principle:** Simplicity beats features. The focus is on a minimalist, high-utility interface for non-technical users.
    
- **Development Methodology:** Spec-driven development utilizing a 5-phase testable build process.
    
- **Communication Style:** "Loud AI"—all outputs should include educational annotations that explain the logic behind the metrics and design choices.
    
- **Branding Constraints:** Strictly "Zero-Branding." All previous "Hoot the Owl" mascot imagery and avian metaphors are removed in favor of a clean, utilitarian aesthetic.

3. The "Data Five" Pulse (Gatekeeper Metrics)

The dashboard's intelligence is built on five critical data pillars used to forecast regional volume:

1. **Hwy 6 (Wilson River) Status:** ODOT condition logs. This is the primary gatekeeper; restricted conditions project volume at only 40% of normal.
    
2. **Gas Price Index:** Tracking regional fuel trends via AAA Oregon to gauge traveler sentiment.
    
3. **Search Interest:** Monitoring Google Search Trends for "Cape Kiwanda" and "Pacific City" to measure upcoming demand.
    
4. **TLT Pulse:** Analyzing historical Tillamook County Transient Lodging Tax reports to identify seasonal performance patterns.
    
5. **OCHI Master Multiplier:** A synthesized forecast (0.00 – 1.00) that provides a single-glance indicator of expected hospitality volume.
    

#{1,6} 4. Technical Architecture (The Google Stack)

The project utilizes a fully integrated Google ecosystem for data processing and visualization:

- **Google Jules:** For Python-based data scraping and BigQuery pipeline engineering.
    
- **Google Sheets & BigQuery:** Serving as the central data warehouse for storing and processing the "Data Five" metrics.
    
- **Looker Studio:** The primary visualization layer for the final dashboard.
    
- **Google Stitch:** Used for mobile-first "Vibe Design" and UI/UX prototyping.

5. Immediate Technical Objectives

- **Data Schema Definition:** Establish the exact columns and fetching logic for all five pillars.
    
- **Mobile-First Layout:** Prioritize a vertical, single-scroll interface with large touch targets for on-site hospitality staff.
    
- **Logic Verification:** Implement testable "Red/Green" pulse checks within the prototype to ensure the OCHI Multiplier accurately reflects real-time gatekeeper changes.
